headers.subject: Fwd: Property Value & CA Real Estate Law

subject: Fwd: Property Value & CA Real Estate Law
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Patricia Hudnall <patriciahudnall@gmail.com>
Date: Wed, Oct 25, 2023 at 7:31 PM
Subject: Property Value & CA Real Estate Law
To: Don Gohore Be Huszar <antonio.hudnall@gmail.com>


I reviewed property sales activity and found the following:

   1. 500 lots sold in the last 12 months

   2. 485 lots sold in the last 6 months - June was a bang up month for
   sales. The prices were all over the place.

   3. 57 lots in the last 3 months. Generally, an appraiser will look at
   the last 90 days for comparable sales. What is most interesting is only 4
   of the 57 properties were less than one acre. These four properties, based
   on their sizing and pricing, result in the comparative properties.


What I cannot tell you is which properties, if any, have an inground well
and/or septic. This information is not included in the public record. There
is also the high probability that these facilities were not installed.

The attached Vacant Land Purchase Agreement lays out for  you the amount of
information considered in a sale. Additionally, beyond the purchase, is the
cost of inspections of both the well and septic. This cost is unknown but
I'm sure that there is a company in Alturas that performs this type of work.

In reviewing the contract, I noted that you preparing to pay the full $8500
with a credit card. If you didn't agree to this, I recommend that you
review the contract in total and ensure that it aligns with the California
Real Estate Law guidelines (attached Vacant Land Purchase Offer).

The property profile confirms the owner's name and contact information.
Let's discuss sometime tomorrow afternoon when you have time.

Sincerely,

Pat

-- 

*Patricia Hudnall,  Realtor *
CA BRE# 01934602
Investment Property Specialist
Probate & Trust Specialist
First Time Buyer Specialist
